validation_checks: willingness to pay or strong usage pull
mvp_defaults: manual or concierge proof before automation
red_flags: feature list without a painful problem
no believable path to first conversations

validation_checks: current spreadsheet, manual, or incumbent workaround
mvp_defaults: thin web app or no-code workflow
manual backend if it proves demand faster
red_flags: heavy platform build before proving one workflow

validation_checks: why onchain state, wallets, ownership, settlement, identity, or composability is necessary
contract, custody, oracle, bridge, and permission risks
token or incentive design if present
ecosystem fit, sponsor fit, and comparable protocols
mvp_defaults: use testnet or localnet until value is proven
separate read-only research from wallet or paid actions
ship a verifier, receipt, or narrow settlement primitive before a full protocol
red_flags: private keys or paid actions without explicit gates

validation_checks: latency and cost per completed task
mvp_defaults: small eval set before broader launch

validation_checks: distribution of the resulting model, notebook, or report

red_flags: pitch does not match prize criteria
operations too heavy for the team

validation_checks: specific customer pain and current workaround
evidence that people want this: users, usage, revenue, LOIs, pilots, waitlist quality, or credible founder access
speed of execution and recent progress
sharp differentiation from incumbents and adjacent startups
program fit: YC, Techstars, 500 Global, Antler, Entrepreneur First, Sequoia Arc, or another target
mvp_defaults: working demo or concierge proof before polished deck
clear traction metric or learning milestone
application answers written plainly, with no marketing-speak
red_flags: vague idea with no user evidence
founder story does not explain why this team should win
application answers that flatter the idea instead of proving it

validation_checks: audience clarity: hackathon judges, investors, angels, accelerators, grants, or partners
solution clarity with demo or product proof
traction or evidence appropriate to stage
deck length and density fit the audience
mvp_defaults: hackathon deck: demo-first, 6 to 8 slides, working proof over market theory
investor deck: venture narrative, 10 to 12 slides, market, traction, business model, team, ask
angel deck: concise 5 to 8 slides, founder story, proof, simple terms, specific use of funds
accelerator deck: execution speed, founder edge, evidence, interview risks
red_flags: generic startup deck that ignores audience
too many slides before the product/demo
ask is missing, vague, or disconnected from milestones
visual polish hiding a weak story
